headline: Council's Rubbish
subtext: Ratepayers funding excessive noise levels at public events.
body:
Wacko Beach - A recent meeting in the Wacko Beach Council Chambers erupted into chaos when Councillor Crappy McSourface suggested replacing all local "Trash" with more eco-friendly options. The move was met with fierce resistance from a "Local Ratepayer," who declared, "I'd rather see our beautiful beaches covered in Rubbish than have anything to do with those tree-hugging wackos!" 

The heated debate spilled out into the streets of Wacko Beach as protestors marched from The Steyne to The Corso, chanting slogans such as "Garbage not guilt" and "Rubbish for a clean beach." 

Wacko Beach's beloved B-Line bus service was also thrown into disarray when its regular driver fell ill. In his absence, the replacement bus driver insisted on taking a more scenic route through The Gap, causing considerable delays in the already disrupted day.

In an attempt to restore some semblance of order, Wacko Beach Council quickly convened another emergency meeting at the Council Chambers. However, with tensions running high and tempers flaring, it remains to be seen if any concrete decisions will come out of these discussions. For now, the residents of Wacko Beach remain divided over how best to handle their Rubbish problem.

In related news, local dog walkers report an unprecedented number of Footpaths sightings in the area surrounding The Corso.

professor.title: From The Archives
professor.content: The archives are currently closed due to a flea infestation.

mystic.prediction: "Capricorn: Tread lightly around The B-Line Bus, as unexpected delays may hinder your progress. Leverage your innate real estate knowledge to navigate property transactions successfully, but remember that surf and coffee breaks should be balanced with your work-life commitments."
